rules.description: Flushing the INPUT chain removes all inbound firewall rules — system becomes accessible to all traffic
rules.message: Flushing iptables INPUT chain is forbidden — this would expose the system to all inbound traffic. Use targeted rule deletion instead.

rules.description: Setting loopback interface down breaks all local services and DNS
rules.message: Disabling the loopback interface (lo) is forbidden — it breaks local sockets, DNS resolution, and all services bound to 127.0.0.1.

rules.description: Removing the default route cuts off all outbound internet access
rules.message: Removing the default route is forbidden — it would cut off all outbound internet access and make the system unreachable remotely.

rules.description: Disabling UFW removes all firewall protection
rules.message: Disabling or resetting UFW is forbidden — it removes all firewall protection. Use targeted rule modifications instead.

rules.description: Any iptables rule modification requires human approval
rules.message: iptables rule modification requires explicit approval — incorrect rules can lock you out or expose services.

rules.description: UFW allow/deny rule changes require approval
rules.message: UFW rule modification requires explicit approval — ensure the change is intentional and reviewed.

rules.description: Setting a non-loopback interface down requires approval
rules.message: Bringing a network interface down requires approval — it will interrupt all traffic on that interface.

rules.description: SSH tunnels to external hosts require approval
rules.message: SSH tunnel requires approval — tunnels bypass firewall rules and forward traffic to external hosts.

rules.description: nmap network scans have legal implications and require explicit authorization
rules.message: nmap scan requires explicit approval — unauthorized port scanning may be illegal. Ensure you have written authorization for the target.

rules.description: iptables modifications require dry-run before execution
rules.message: Dry-run required for iptables modifications — shows current rule set before change.

rules.description: Route modifications require dry-run to show current routing table
rules.message: Dry-run required for route changes — shows current routing table before modification.

rules.description: DNS lookup tools are always safe to run
rules.message: DNS query — read-only, safe to execute.

rules.description: Network status tools (netstat, ss, ping, traceroute) are read-only
rules.message: Network status check — read-only, safe to execute.

rules.description: ip addr/link/route show commands are read-only
rules.message: ip show command — read-only, safe to execute.
